2603.26182.title: ClinicalAgents: Multi-Agent Orchestration for Clinical Decision Making with Dual-Memory
2603.26182.paper_summary: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated potential in healthcare, they often struggle with the complex, non-linear reasoning required for accurate clinical diagnosis. Existing methods typically rely on static, linear mappings from symptoms to diagnoses, failing to capture the iterative, hypothesis-driven reasoning inherent to human clinicians. To bridge this gap, we introduce ClinicalAgents, a novel multi-agent framework designed to simulate the cognitive workflow of expert clinicians. Unlike rigid sequential chains, ClinicalAgents employs a dynamic orchestration mechanism modeled as a Monte Carlo Tree Search (MCTS) process. This allows an Orchestrator to iteratively generate hypotheses, actively verify evidence, and trigger backtracking when critical information is missing. Central to this framework is a Dual-Memory architecture: a mutable Working Memory that maintains the evolving patient state for context-aware reasoning, and a static Experience Memory that retrieves clinical guidelines and historical cases via an active feedback loop. Extensive experiments demonstrate that ClinicalAgents achieves state-of-the-art performance, significantly enhancing both diagnostic accuracy and explainability compared to strong single-agent and multi-agent baselines.
2603.26182.paper_summary_zh: 雖然大型語言模型（LLMs）在醫療保健中顯示出潛力，但它們在準確的臨床診斷所需的複雜非線性推理方面往往面臨挑戰。現有的方法通常依賴於從症狀到診斷的靜態線性映射，未能捕捉到人類臨床醫生固有的迭代假設驅動推理。為了彌補這一差距，我們引入了ClinicalAgents，一個新穎的多代理框架，旨在模擬專家臨床醫生的認知工作流程。與僵化的序列鏈不同，ClinicalAgents採用動態編排機制，建模為蒙特卡羅樹搜索（MCTS）過程。這使得一個編排者能夠迭代生成假設，主動驗證證據，並在關鍵信息缺失時觸發回溯。這一框架的核心是一個雙記憶體架構：一個可變的工作記憶，維護不斷演變的患者狀態以進行上下文感知推理，以及一個靜態的經驗記憶，通過主動反饋循環檢索臨床指導方針和歷史案例。大量實驗表明，ClinicalAgents在性能上達到了最先進的水平，顯著提高了診斷準確性和可解釋性，相較於強大的單代理和多代理基準。
2603.26182.author: Zhuohan Ge et.al.
2603.26182.authors: Zhuohan Ge, Haoyang Li, Yubo Wang, Nicole Hu, Chen Jason Zhang, Qing Li
